question: [File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user craft API requests that each hold a database connection open for the full `SetConnMaxLifetime` duration (30 minutes by default), causing the pool to exhaust `SetMaxOpenConns(dbConfig.Pool.MaxOpenConnections)` slots and forcing the node to queue or block new DB work, increasing CPU and memory consumption by over 30%?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user send a moderate number of concurrent Rosetta API requests that each trigger a slow database query, exhausting the `MaxOpenConnections` pool limit set via `sqlDb.SetMaxOpenConns(dbConfig.Pool.MaxOpenConnections)`, causing goroutine accumulation and memory growth exceeding 30% above baseline?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: GetDbWithContext()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger the `statementTimeout <= 0` branch in `GetDbWithContext` by exploiting a misconfigured or zero-value `StatementTimeout`, causing queries to run indefinitely and hold DB connections open, increasing node resource consumption by 30% or more?
